steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The table shows tribal economic activity has the fewest employees (51,674) but \$7.3 billion in revenue. The question asks which claim is supported by the data, so compare figures across all columns.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — made the lowest total contribution by industry.: Retail (\$10.7B) exceeds tribal (\$7.3B). ✗
  • Choice B — ranked highest in all three economic measures listed in the table.: Tribal has the fewest employees at 51,674. ✗
  • Choice C — made the highest average economic contribution per employee.: \$7.3B ÷ 51,674 = ~\$141,510 per employee, well above retail (~\$59,924) and accommodation/food services (~\$34,861). ✓
  • Choice D — employed the same number of people as accommodation/food services.: The table shows 51,674, not 150,373. ✗
That is the correct answer.
